plugin.name: Jujutsu VCS Integration
plugin.description:
Native IntelliJ integration for Jujutsu (jj), a Git-compatible version control system with a fundamentally different approach to working with code.

Why Jujutsu?

Jujutsu treats your working copy as a commit—always. No staging area, no "dirty" state. Just describe what you're working on and keep coding.

Key Features

  • Describe-First Workflow — Working Copy tool window for describing current work and creating new changes.
  • Custom Log View — Visual commit graph with inline change IDs, descriptions, and bookmarks.
  • Change Operations — Edit, abandon, and describe changes directly from the log.
  • File History & Annotations — Full file history with diff viewer and line-by-line blame.
  • Multi-Repository Support — Work with multiple JJ repositories in a single project.
  • Real-Time Status — Auto-refresh keeps the UI in sync as you edit files.

GitHub · Issues
plugin.change-notes:
0.7.14

Fixed

  • The plugin no longer bundles its own copy of the Kotlin standard library, reducing plugin size since the IDE already provides it. ([#41](https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/41))
  • Annotate (blame) no longer logs a spurious error when the project window is closed while an annotation is loading. ([#45](https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/45))
  • File-change actions (Open, Compare, Restore, etc.) no longer throw an error when a change from another VCS (e.g. Git in a colocated repository, or an unrelated VCS backend) is selected alongside or instead of a Jujutsu change. ([#50](https://github.com/kkkev/jj-idea/issues/50))

    • See full release notes for details.
